label: tactical_analysis
text: Using a high press against a team with this much technical quality was a gamble that didn't pay off. They just played right through us.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The striker’s hold-up play was the key. He occupied both center backs, allowing the wingers to make those diagonal runs inside.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Looking at the pass network, the connection between the CM and RW was our most productive route. We should have exploited it more.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The transition defense was non-existent. We were left with 2 defenders against 4 attackers every time we lost the ball.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Our xG per shot was extremely low today. We were just hitting speculative long shots instead of working the ball into the box.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The movement off the ball was tragic. Everyone was just standing still waiting for the ball to come to them. No rotations at all.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Possession was 50-50 but they had 10 more shots. They were just way more efficient at moving the ball forward quickly.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Their press was triggered every time our LB touched the ball. They clearly identified him as the weak link in our build-up.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Argentina [3] - 0 Algeria - Lionel Messi 76‎'‎ hat-trick

label: tactical_analysis
text: Saliba won 100% of his aerial duels today. France’s aerial dominance is why they haven't conceded from a set piece yet.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Spain’s false nine system failed today because there were 0 runs in behind to stretch the center-backs.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Norway's Erling Haaland is the first player to score twice on their World Cup debut since

label: tactical_analysis
text: The stats show that 80% of the chances came from the left flank where Davies was doubling up with the winger.

label: tactical_analysis
text: For the first time since 1930, the USMNT has won back-to-back games in the men’s World Cup.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Japan’s defensive block was so compact that the opposition only managed 2 touches in the penalty area all game.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Shocking amount of people think cutting across a defender from their blindside and going down is a foul

label: tactical_analysis
text: Portugal’s average position map shows a massive hole in the pivot; they miss Palhinha’s defensive coverage.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Bellingham’s pass completion under pressure was 94% today—he’s literally the only reason England kept the ball.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The high press was elite today. We forced 15 turnovers in their defensive third, which is the most I’ve seen from this squad in years.

label: tactical_analysis
text: People are hating on the CDM but his positioning was 10/10. He blocked the passing lanes to their #10 all night, basically taking him out of the game.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Our build-up play was way too slow. By the time the ball reached the final third, they already had 10 men behind the ball in a low block.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The double pivot was a disaster tbh. Neither of them wanted to drop deep to collect the ball, so the CBs were just forced to spam long balls.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Rodri’s pass completion under pressure was 98% today. It’s actually insane how he dictates the tempo while 3 players are hounding him.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The decision to play an inverted left-back was genius. It allowed us to have a numerical advantage in midfield during the transition phase.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Their PPDA was under 7 today. They didn't let us breathe for a second. That level of pressing intensity is impossible to play against.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Tactical masterclass from the manager. Using a false nine pulled their captain out of position and opened up the massive gap for the winner.

label: tactical_analysis
text: We conceded because the wingers weren't tracking back. The fullbacks were constantly left in 2v1 situations on the overlap.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Musiala’s dribbling stats are elite, but his decision-making in the final third was poor today. Too many touches instead of the simple pass.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The stats show we had 70% possession but 0 shots on target in the first half. Pure 'U-shape' passing with no verticality at all.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Their keeper's distribution is a joke. He started 3 of their best chances just by pinging 50-yard balls directly to the wingers' feet.

label: tactical_analysis
text: We finally saw some tactical flexibility. Dropping into a mid-block after the first goal completely neutralized their pace on the counter.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The average position map is hilarious. Our strikers were basically standing on each other's toes. Zero width in the final third.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Bellingham playing as a shadow striker worked because he drew the defenders away, leaving the actual striker 1v1 with the keeper.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Defensively, we were so compact. The distance between our front line and back line was never more than 20 yards. Elite discipline.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Their #6 was cooked by the 60th minute. He couldn't keep up with the rotations in our midfield and started leaving huge gaps.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The xA from our right back today was higher than the rest of the team combined. His crossing is our only real threat.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Japan’s transition from defense to attack is the fastest in the tournament. 3 passes and they’re in the box every single time.

label: tactical_analysis
text: We lost the tactical battle in the half-spaces. Their #8s were constantly finding pockets of space between our midfield and defense.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Playing with two holding midfielders was a mistake. We had zero creativity in the final third and the striker was completely isolated.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The heatmap shows we spent the entire game in their half, but we only had 1 big chance created. We need a proper poacher.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Their high line is so risky. One long ball over the top and they’re completely exposed. They got lucky with the offside calls today.

text: Cristiano Ronaldo has gone 10 consecutive World Cup and European Championship matches without scoring

text: USA's 4-3-3 transition into a 3-2-5 in possession completely overwhelmed the Australian low block today.

text: Musiala’s heatmap is insane; he spent 40% of the match in the left half-space, dragging the fullback out of position.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Statistically, Brazil's PPDA was 7.2 today, their highest pressing intensity since the 2022 qualifiers.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The decision to play a high line against Mbappe was suicide; he had 5 progressive carries into the box in the first half alone.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Looking at the xG map, Mexico had 2.4 xG but only 1 big chance created. Too many low-probability long shots.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Argentina is struggling because Enzo is dropping too deep to pick up the ball, leaving Messi isolated between the lines.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Italy’s 'box midfield' created a 4v3 overload centrally, making it impossible for the midfield to progress the ball.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Playing with two holding midfielders was a mistake. We had zero creativity in the final third and the striker was completely isolated.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The heatmap shows we spent the entire game in their half, but we only had 1 big chance created. We need a proper poacher.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Their high line is so risky. One long ball over the top and they’re completely exposed. They got lucky with the offside calls today.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Statistically, Rice made 6 interceptions in the first half. His ability to read the game and break up play is literally world class.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The substitution changed everything. Bringing on a traditional winger meant we finally had someone to stretch their back five.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Our press was disjointed. The front three went, but the midfield didn't follow, leaving a massive hole for them to play through.

label: tactical_analysis
text: We’re playing too many sideways passes. We need someone in the pivot who is brave enough to play those line-breaking balls.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Man-marking their #6 out of the game was a masterclass; he didn't complete a single progressive pass in the entire second half.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Switching from a 4-3-3 to a 3-4-3 during possession helped create the wide overloads we needed to break their low block.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Their captain was man-marking our best player out of the game. He didn't have a single touch in the box for 90 minutes.

label: tactical_analysis
text: The tactical foul on the 80th minute was smart. It stopped a 3v1 counter and allowed the defense to reset. Professional play.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Look at the xG breakdown—we had 15 shots but almost all of them were from outside the box with a probability under 0.05.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Average positions from the match show a massive gap between the midfield and the strikers, which is why we looked so disjointed.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Every time we tried to play out from the back, their front three triggered a coordinated press that forced our keeper into long balls.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Using an inverted left-back allowed us to dominate the central areas, but it left the flank wide open for their counter-attacks.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Honestly, playing such a high defensive line against a team with that much pace was just asking for trouble.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Ngl, switching to a double pivot in the 60th minute was the only thing that stopped us from getting completely overrun in midfield.

label: tactical_analysis
text: Saka’s heatmap shows he was basically playing as a wing-back today, which explains why we had zero threat in the final third.
